sourceMovie.title: The Florida Project
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Florida Project for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Sean Baker keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Moonlight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Florida Project clicked for you, Moonlight (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bridge to Terabithia
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Florida Project clicked for you, Bridge to Terabithia (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Florida Project?
faq.answer: Try Moonlight, Bridge to Terabithia, Rocky IV—each shares DNA with The Florida Project while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Florida Project?
faq.answer: The Florida Project (2017) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Florida Project worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
